name: Trail of the Towers
name_original: De Villadiego a Olmos de la Picaza — circular por el Páramo de Villadiego
description: Hike a moderate 8.8-mile (14.2 km) circular route from Villadiego to Olmos de la Picaza, exploring Castilian plains and historic landmarks.
region_intro: Hiking around Villadiego offers access to the diverse natural landscapes of the Odra-Pisuerga region in Burgos, Spain. The area features prominent natural monuments such as Peña Amaya, which reaches 1,370 meters, and several waterfalls including La Cañada Waterfall and La Fuentona. Hikers can explore river canyons like the Rudrón Canyon and Ebro Canyon, which provide varied terrain for outdoor activities. The region's trails often combine natural beauty with historical sites. Best hiking trails around Villadiego The most popular hiking route is Climb to Peña Amaya and visit its Cave — circular through Valdelucio lands, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 46 minutes to complete. This moderate route ascends to the prominent Peña Amaya, offering expansive views. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Cañada Waterfall – La Fuentona loop from El Vaho, a moderate 9.1 miles (14.7 km) path. This trail explores notable waterfalls and scenic landscapes around La Fuentona. Local hikers also love the Trail of the Towers, a 8.8 miles (14.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 43 minutes. Hiking around Villadiego is defined by prominent peaks like Peña Amaya, scenic waterfalls, and river canyons. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Villadiego are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 18 reviews. More than 60 hikers have used komoot to explore Villadiego's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Villadiego?
A: There are over 9 hiking routes around Villadiego, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These include easy walks, moderate trails, and some more challenging ascents.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for hiking trails in Villadiego?
A: The trails around Villadiego cater to various abilities. You'll find 2 easy routes, 6 moderate routes, and 1 difficult route, ensuring options for both casual walkers and experienced hikers.

Q: What natural landmarks can I explore while hiking in Villadiego?
A: The region is rich in natural landmarks. You can visit prominent features like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Amaya&lt;/a&gt;, a significant summit offering expansive views. Other notable natural monuments include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4382544" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fuentona&lt;/a&gt;, an upwelling of water, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3066344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cucarada Cave&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on hikes near Villadiego?
A: Yes, the area features several beautiful waterfalls. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3066377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cañada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, near Hoyos del Tozo, is easily accessible and known for its significant flow. The Rudrón Canyon, close to Tubilla del Agua, is also recognized for its collection of waterfalls and excellent hiking trails.

Q: Are there historical sites or cultural attractions near the trails?
A: Villadiego itself is a historic town, and the surrounding area blends natural beauty with historical sites. You can explore &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4381459" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CastroAmaya&lt;/a&gt;, a historical site, or consider routes that pass through culturally rich areas. Orbaneja del Castillo, a town declared a Heritage of Cultural Interest, is also a must-visit nearby.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Villadiego?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience around Villadiego, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 18 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, including prominent peaks, scenic waterfalls, and river canyons, as well as the combination of natural beauty with historical sites.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in Villadiego?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as circular paths. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046650221/climb-to-pena-amaya-and-visit-its-cave-circular-through-valdelucio-lands" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Peña Amaya and visit its Cave — circular through Valdelucio lands&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047058710/castillo-de-urbel-circular-by-la-piedra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Urbel — circular by La Piedra&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a loop around a historical site.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Villadiego?
A: The spring season is particularly recommended for hiking in Villadiego, as the waterfalls are at their most impressive, and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Autumn also offers pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful foliage.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Villadiego?
A: Many natural areas in Spain are dog-friendly, but it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Villadiego?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always available, the region offers several easy and moderate routes that could be suitable for families. Look for trails with shorter distances and less elevation gain, such as some of the easy routes available, to ensure a comfortable experience for all ages.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming near Villadiego's hiking trails?
A: The region features rivers and waterfalls, such as La Fuentona and the Rudrón Canyon, which might offer spots for refreshing dips. However, always exercise caution, check local regulations, and be aware of water conditions and safety before attempting to swim in natural bodies of water.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails?
A: The terrain around Villadiego is diverse, ranging from paths along river canyons like the Ebro Canyon and Rudrón Canyon, to ascents up prominent peaks such as Peña Amaya. You can expect a mix of forest paths, open fields, and rocky sections, often combining natural beauty with historical elements.
tips: In and around Olmos de la Picaza, you can enjoy outdoor activities such as hiking and cycling. In addition, the region has several points of historical and cultural interest, such as churches and traditional buildings that reflect the typical architecture of the area.

An extensive plain appears that goes from the town of Villadiego and extends for several kilometers in an east direction. There are a set of paths to get to know this flat place, surrounded by mountains.

The Villadiego bridge is an example of civil architecture that is made up of three arches, the central one being higher and wider than the side ones. It is made of stone, its construction is ashlar and it has a vaulted structure.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the From Villadiego to Olmos de la Picaza - circular via the Paramo de Villadiego trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as moderate. While it features mostly accessible paths, it requires good physical fitness due to its length and a total elevation gain and loss of approximately 120 meters.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is a circular path, starting and ending in the same general area, offering a varied experience without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is characteristic of the 'Páramos y Campos' region in Burgos, featuring high plains and agricultural fields, primarily for cereal cultivation. You can expect expansive views of the Castilian meseta and will traverse a gently descending valley flanked by 'cuestas' (slopes or hills).

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or points of interest can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You will encounter several interesting features, including the Tower of Olmos de la Picaza, a significant 14th or 15th-century stately tower. The route also passes by a Medieval bridge over the Brullés River (Villadiego) and offers views of the Plain near Olmos de la Picaza.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails, including the Sendero de los Torreones (77% of the route), Camino Castromorca (22%), and smaller portions of R13 - Riberas del Odra, R14 - Las Loras, R15 - Los llanos de Villadiego, and R16 - Ribera del Brullés.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Villadiego. As a historic town, Villadiego offers various parking options, typically in or near the town center, which serves as a convenient starting point for this circular route.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions mentioned, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock, and to ensure you clean up after them. The open terrain is suitable for walks with well-behaved dogs.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Castilian plateau can experience hot summers and cold winters. Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking, offering milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Consider the lack of shade in the open 'páramo' landscape when planning for warmer months.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, Villadiego is a historic town and offers amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options, making it a good base for your hike. Olmos de la Picaza is a smaller locality with fewer services.

faqs.question: What is a good strategy for hiking this trail, considering its characteristics?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's moderate difficulty and open 'páramo' landscape, it's wise to start early, especially on warmer days, to avoid the midday sun as shade can be limited. Bringing sufficient water and sun protection is highly recommended. Starting in Villadiego allows easy access to amenities before and after your hike.
